behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior across directory hierarchy. MOCK-MISS: this is not just local parsing logic; it depends on watcher-to-pipeline handoff for nested paths. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can miss OS-specific recursive watch semantics and event delivery differences. Rejection-gate for higher levels: no behavior text indicates AI/ML output or multi-agent sequencing, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temp directory tree. Create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file there, and assert one transformed output appears in the output directory with the expected relative path mapping.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text centers on "deduplication logic" for same path + time window, which is a local algorithm/state check. MOCK-MISS: no required module boundary in the behavior itself. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess dependency is required to trigger the failure. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed; (b) no AI/ML dependence; (c) no multi-agent chain; (d) no LLM stage handoff. Rejection-gate for higher levels: text does not require watcher events, only dedup window semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the dedup component with a fake/injected clock, submit the same canonical file path twice within the configured window, assert second request is rejected; advance clock beyond window and assert request is accepted.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test remains fully in-process and validates only the dedup boundary.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says "operating system emits multiple filesystem events," so failure boundary is OS event behavior. REAL-INFRA: real FS events are required. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher events may not reproduce duplicate burst timing/order seen in real kernels/backends. Rejection-gate for higher levels: no AI/ML decision content and no multi-agent pipeline, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real watched directory. Perform a single write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp-write+rename or rapid append/close) and assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is watcher lifecycle under real directory deletion/recreation events. REAL-INFRA: requires OS filesystem state transitions and backend watcher re-registration behavior. MOCK-HIDE: a fake watcher can mask race conditions and missed re-subscriptions. Rejection-gate for higher levels: behavior does not involve AI/ML output quality or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real temp watched directory, delete that directory while running, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Behavior couples arrival concurrency with file creation timestamps, which depend on real filesystem metadata resolution and event timing. MOCK-MISS: ordering bug can appear at watcher->queue handoff under concurrent arrivals. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes often impose deterministic ordering that hides real timestamp/event races. Rejection-gate for higher levels: no AI/ML dependence or multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real filesystem, create multiple files near-simultaneously with controlled creation times (or staggered writes), capture processing order from daemon logs/outputs, and assert it matches creation timestamp order with defined tie-break behavior.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real file-write lifecycle vs watcher event timing. REAL-INFRA: requires actual partial writes/open handles/close semantics. MOCK-HIDE: stubs usually model writes atomically and hide premature pickup bugs. Rejection-gate for higher levels: behavior text has no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-step agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real watched directory. Open a file for writing, write partial content, keep handle open while triggering arrival, verify daemon does not process yet; close writer, then verify processing occurs once with full final content.
